On this page
  • get_sensor_covariance()
  • _get_bio_channel_type_idx()

  • _calc_sensor_noise_cov()

Sensor covariance¶

This module may be employed to calculate the sensor covariance matrix.

source_reconstruction.sensor_covariance.get_sensor_covariance(file_path, cov_path, method=None, method_params=None, overwrite=False)¶

#Determines eigenvectors/values from the sensor noise covariance

Parameters:

  • file_path – The (empty room) file to use for sensor noise covariance calculations. Important: Evaluate a number of different files to identify a good example.

  • cov_path – Path to a the covariance file. If none exists, the covariance will be saved in this location.

  • method – Method to be employed, either “empirically”, “shrinkage”, or “factor_analysis” (default: shrinkage).

  • method_params – Method specific parameters. Only applies to sklearn.covariance.ShrunkCovariance and sklearn.decomposition.FactorAnalysis (default: {“shrinkage” : 0.2,} - epoch size in s).

  • overwrite – Flag to overwrite covariance calculation.

Returns:

(cov, ch_names) - Covariance and channel names.

source_reconstruction.sensor_covariance._get_bio_channel_type_idx(raw_file, mask=None)¶
source_reconstruction.sensor_covariance._empirically_estimate_cov(cov_data, meg_ch_indices, grad_ch_indices, valid_ch_indices)¶
source_reconstruction.sensor_covariance._calc_sensor_noise_cov(raw_file, method=None, method_params=None, epoch_sz_s=0.2)¶

Calculates the sensor noise covariance

Parameters:

  • raw_file – The (empty room) file to use for sensor noise covariance calculations. Important: Evaluate a number of different files to identify a good example.

  • method – Method to be employed, either “empirically”, “shrinkage”, or “factor_analysis”.

  • method_params – Method specific parameters. Only applies to sklearn.covariance.ShrunkCovariance and sklearn.decomposition.FactorAnalysis.

  • epoch_sz_s – Epoch size for covariance calculation in seconds.

Returns:

(cov, ch_names) - Covariance and channel names.
